programs.name: Missouri Rx Program (MoRx)
programs.what_it_does: This state-funded program assists Missouri seniors on Medicare Part D with prescription drug costs by covering most common medications. It helps reduce out-of-pocket expenses for eligible participants through state assistance beyond federal coverage.
programs.who_its_for: Missouri residents aged 65+ or those 18+ with disabilities, enrolled in Medicare Part D, meeting state-specific income limits.
programs.whats_unique: State-funded pharmaceutical assistance program supplementing Medicare Part D, not available in every state; served over 50,000 seniors in 2024 with average annual savings of $200.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Average annual savings of $200 per participant.

programs.name: Missouri Property Tax Credit (Circuit Breaker)
programs.what_it_does: This program provides refunds to eligible low-income seniors for a portion of property taxes paid or rent constituting property taxes. It offers direct financial relief to help seniors afford housing costs in Missouri.
programs.who_its_for: Missouri residents aged 65+ (or 18+ with disabilities), meeting income limits, who are homeowners or renters.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific property tax relief program enacted by Missouri statute, providing up to $1,100 for homeowners and $750 for renters; over 100,000 seniors claimed it in 2023—not a federal standard.[1][4]
programs.benefit_value: Up to $1,100 for homeowners or $750 for renters.

programs.name: Aged and Disabled Waiver (ADW)
programs.what_it_does: This Medicaid HCBS waiver delivers home and community-based services like adult day care, homemaker services, home-delivered meals, chore services, and respite care. It aims to help participants aged 63+ avoid nursing home placement by supporting independent living.
programs.who_its_for: Missouri residents aged 63+ at risk of institutionalization, eligible for MO HealthNet (Missouri Medicaid), with chronic or disabling conditions confirmed via functional needs assessment.
programs.whats_unique: Missouri's state-administered Medicaid waiver with specific services like advanced respite care and chore services (e.g., rodent control); state chooses to offer this optional federal framework with tailored benefits.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Varies; e.g., up to 10 hours/day adult day health care (5 days/week), up to 2 home-delivered meals/day, in-home respite.

programs.name: Senior Independent Living Program (SILP)
programs.what_it_does: This state program supports independent living for seniors through community-based services. It faced budget cuts but remains a Missouri-specific initiative for older adults.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Missouri seniors aged 60+ needing assistance to remain independent at home.
programs.whats_unique: Missouri state-funded program for senior independent living, distinct from federal Older Americans Act services; noted for budget impacts unique to state fiscal policies.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by service; community-based supports (specific amounts not detailed in sources).
